content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
Phone Memo 
Date 
: December 17,1975 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
TED PRIM 
Subject: 
December 9, 1975 t phone conversation with Yvonne Smith, Registrar 
in San Diego. 
Ms. Smith called me with the following question: 
A committee organized under Waxman Dymally had surplus funds 
of $8,000 on January 6, 1975. 
Since January 6, the committee 
has not received any contributions nor made any expenditures. 
Ms. Smith asked if the committee were required to file a 
statement of organization pursuant to Government Code Section 
84101 which states "every committee which is a committee 
by virtue of Section 82013(a) shall file with the Secretary 
of State a statement of organization within ten days after 
it is formed as a committee. 
Each such committee in existence 
at the date of enact~ent of this chapter shall file a statement 
with the Secretary of State ,,rithin thirty days after the 
effective date of this chapter" 
EmphasiS Added. 
I told her that the committee in question need not file 
a statement of organization so long as it did not receive contributions 
in the amount of $500 or more ~uring the 1975 calendar year. 
It is my understanding that committees must qualify anew each 
year. 
The reference in Section 84101 to committees in existence 
merely afforded operating committees a thirty day period to file 
a statement of organization rather than the ten day period prescribed 
for newly formed committees, 
This provision does not require 
that every committee holding surplus funds on January 7, 1975 
must necessarily file a statement of organization,

sections.question: A committee organized under Waxman Dymally had surplus funds of $8,000 on January 6, 1975. Since January 6, the committee has not received any contributions nor made any expenditures. Is the committee required to file a statement of organization pursuant to Government Code Section 84101?
sections.conclusion: The committee in question need not file a statement of organization so long as it did not receive contributions in the amount of $500 or more during the 1975 calendar year. Committees must qualify anew each year. The reference in Section 84101 to committees in existence merely afforded operating committees a thirty day period to file a statement of organization rather than the ten day period prescribed for newly formed committees. This provision does not require that every committee holding surplus funds on January 7, 1975 must necessarily file a statement of organization.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a committee with $8,000 in surplus funds, which has received no contributions or made expenditures since January 6, 1975, must file a statement of organization under Government Code Section 84101.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The committee need not file a statement of organization as long as it does not receive $500 or more in contributions during the 1975 calendar year. The thirty-day filing period for existing committees applies only to those that become operative, not to every committee holding surplus funds.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a phone memo/advice letter hybrid dated December 17, 1975. No formal CONCLUSION header present, but advice is clearly given in final paragraph. Question derived from narrated phone conversation. Document discusses Government Code Section 84101 filing requirements for political committees.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A committee organized under Waxman Dymally had surplus funds of $8,000 on January 6, 1975. Since January 6, the committee has not received any contributions nor made any expenditures. Is the committee required to file a statement of organization pursuant to Government Code Section 84101?

CONCLUSION: The committee in question need not file a statement of organization so long as it did not receive contributions in the amount of $500 or more during the 1975 calendar year. Committees must qualify anew each year. The reference in Section 84101 to committees in existence merely afforded operating committees a thirty day period to file a statement of organization rather than the ten day period prescribed for newly formed committees. This provision does not require that every committee holding surplus funds on January 7, 1975 must necessarily file a statement of organization.

embedding.summary: FPPC advisor Ted Prim responds to a phone inquiry from San Diego Registrar Yvonne Smith regarding whether a dormant Waxman-Dymally committee with $8,000 in surplus funds must file a statement of organization. The FPPC concludes the committee need not file so long as it receives no contributions of $500 or more in the calendar year.
